_note: No theme at all → cannot resource the principle with puzzles → auto_reject regardless of similarity or frequency.
Empty string theme treated same as no theme.
Theme string that is not in the LICHESS_THEMES set → auto_reject.
CRITICAL: similarity >= SIMILARITY_HIGH (0.80) must NEVER route to auto_approve. A duplicate slipping into the live vocabulary is the worst-case failure.
similarity_score = SIMILARITY_HIGH (0.80 exactly) — boundary: >= check means this routes to human_review, not auto_approve.
Even very high frequency (many users) cannot override the similarity gate — human must review potential duplicates.
similarity < SIMILARITY_LOW (0.60) AND occurrence_count >= MIN_OCCURRENCE (3) AND distinct_user_count >= MIN_DISTINCT_USERS (2) → auto_approve.
similarity = 0.59 (just under SIMILARITY_LOW), occurrence_count = MIN_OCCURRENCE exactly, distinct_user_count = MIN_DISTINCT_USERS exactly → auto_approve.
Good similarity and theme, but occurrence_count (2) < MIN_OCCURRENCE (3) → hold (not enough pattern evidence yet).
Enough occurrences but only 1 distinct user — could be one player's quirk, not a real pattern. distinct_user_count (1) < MIN_DISTINCT_USERS (2) → hold.
similarity_score = SIMILARITY_LOW (0.60 exactly) — the check is `< SIMILARITY_LOW` so 0.60 does NOT satisfy it. Falls into middle band (0.60-0.80) → human_review.
0.60 < similarity < 0.80, has valid theme, freq met — middle band, judgment call → human_review.
